AdamiPad-20260331.optionNote: High-speed, durable external storage for iPad

options.Notes: 10GBPS, 1GBPS rw, IP65 water/dust resistance, rugged performance
options.Further_Notes:
SAMSUNG T7 Shield 2TB SSD, Blue, MU-PE2T0R/AM, T7 Shield SSD : Amazon.com.au
Slower on paper than T9 / SanDisk Extreme PRO, but the iPad generally cannot exploit those 20 Gbps USB 3.2 Gen 2x2 headline speeds anyway.

AdamiPad-20260331.optionNote: Includes pencil holder; safer than mag-charge cases

options.Notes: Protective case with pencil holder, compatible with iPad Pro 13 Inch (M5/M4) (2025/2024), Black
options.Further_Notes: If not going for Apple Magic Keyboards and Case, get a simple one (not a slow usb one or unsafe mag-charge one)

AdamiPad-20260331.optionNote: Needed to be able to power + external storage + keyboard and mouse + external displays,10GBps capable (else bottle-neck ssd performance)

options.optionNote: Needed to be able to power + external storage + keyboard and mouse + external displays,10GBps capable (else bottle-neck ssd performance)

options.description: Satechi 4-Port USB-C Hub with Power Delivery
options.Notes: USB-C only hub; simple layout with equal-throughput USB-C ports and power delivery passthrough
options.Further_Notes: AU$60 plus shipping. Positioned as a more premium third-party brand, commonly sold through retailers like the Apple Store. Good fit if you only need USB-C expansion and do not need HDMI, Ethernet, or mixed legacy ports.

options.Notes: 10 Gbps dock with more expansion for accessories and displays; supports up to 100W power delivery passthrough
options.Further_Notes: Best-featured option here if you want one dock to handle storage, peripherals, charging, and external display connectivity. Considerably more expensive than the simpler hub options.

options.Notes: 8-in-1 USB-C hub with 100W power delivery passthrough and a broader mix of ports than the Satechi
options.Further_Notes: Well-known mainstream accessory brand and materially cheaper than the CalDigit. More versatile than a USB-C-only hub, but still a lower-end choice than CalDigit if dock reliability and display handling are priorities.

options.Notes: 10 Gbps USB-C hub with dual-monitor oriented feature set at a very low price point
options.Further_Notes: Very aggressive pricing for the feature set. Brand is less established and long-term reliability/support is less certain than Satechi, Anker, or CalDigit, so this is the higher-risk budget option.
